data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 42c2-39e1-16cd-455c (CACHE-INTEG), child 7a41-7cc3-62f5-4efa "child one", sibling roster: 6019-d533-79a0-4017 "child two"] Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes between this child and sibling child two:

1. Implicit shared state — neither child references cache/runner-state/shared resource; no shared-state assumption to conflict. Clean.
2. Conflicting assumptions — both are pure placeholders ("a"/"x" vs "b"/"y") asserting nothing that could contradict. Clean.
3. Dependency gap — neither child declares an ordering/dependency; the parent's "later pairings" sequencing is a G3 coverage matter, not a child-vs-child dependency gap. Clean.
4. Scope overlap — Scope "x" vs "y" are distinct opaque tokens with no described overlapping behavior. Clean.
5. Ordering violation — no child states an order; nothing to violate. Clean.
6. Consumer impact — no child describes an interface/output the other consumes. Clean.
7. Residual references — no dangling cross-child references. Clean.

The placeholder-coverage problem (children not mapping to parent criteria) is a G3 concern and is already documented. Applying the high-confidence + benign-reading filter, no cross-child contradiction, overlap, or ordering gap is detectable here.

G4 RESULT: clean pairing — no findings.
